input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, from scratch:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → install the iOS simulator runtime you need.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest MAUI features).
- Use the **macOS installer (`.pkg`)** for ARM64 (Apple Silicon) or x64 (Intel).
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all dependencies.
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify installed workloads:** `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install Java JDK (for Android)

- The MAUI Android workload installs **Microsoft OpenJDK 17** automatically.
- If you need to install manually: [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- Verify: `java --version` (should show JDK 17+)

## 5. Install Android SDK

The **recommended approach** — let the MAUI workload handle it. On first build, it auto-provisions the Android SDK. To manage it explicitly:

- Install **Android Command-Line Tools** from [developer.android.com/studio#command-tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools) (you do **not** need full Android Studio).
- Or install **Android Studio** if you prefer the GUI: [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio).
- Accept licenses:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net8.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- Required components (auto-installed by workload):
  - Android SDK Build-Tools (latest)
  - Android SDK Platform (API 34 or 35)
  - Android Emulator + system images (for testing)

## 6. Discover Correct Versions

| What | How to check |
|---|---|
| Supported .NET versions | [github.com/dotnet/maui — wiki/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Release-Versions) |
| Required Xcode version | `dotnet workload install maui` output, or [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| Required Android API level | `dotnet workload install maui` pulls the right one |
| Installed workloads | `dotnet workload list` |
| Installed SDKs | `dotnet --list-sdks` |

## 7. (Optional) Install an IDE

- **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI Extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — free, lightweight.
- **JetBrains Rider** — full-featured, paid (has built-in MAUI support).
- *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.*

## 8. Create & Verify

```bash
# Create a new MAUI project
dotnet new maui -n MyTestApp
cd MyTestApp

# Build for all targets
dotnet build

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 9. Verification Checklist

```bash
dotnet --version              # .NET 8.0+ or 9.0+
dotnet workload list          # shows "maui" installed
xcodebuild -version           # Xcode 16+
java --version                # JDK 17+
dotnet new maui -n VerifyApp  # succeeds
cd VerifyApp && dotnet build  # builds without errors
```

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with `dotnet new maui`. The workload handles Android SDK + JDK provisioning automatically.
